headline: I started asking where our meat came from. I did not like the answer.
subhead: A letter from our founder on why we built a grocery store where every product has a farm name on it.
hero_copy:
My youngest asked me once where chicken comes from. I said the supermarket. She meant the actual chicken.

I did not know the farm. I did not know how the bird was raised, what it ate, or how far it had travelled to reach our kitchen. I just knew it came in a plastic tray and I bought it every week.

That question sat with me for months. Eventually it became FarmThru.

sections.heading: The problem I could not unsee
sections.body:
Industry data suggests supermarket beef can travel around 2,400 kilometres before reaching the shelf. It might sit in cold storage for up to two or three weeks. The farmer who raised it earns a fraction of what you pay.

Meanwhile, there are regenerative farms right here in NSW producing extraordinary food. Rachel's Farm in Kempsey. Bundarra Farm. Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. Farmer Brown. Why could families like mine not buy from them directly?

sections.heading: So we built a hub
sections.body:
FarmThru connects regenerative farms directly to families through a hub-and-collect model. You order online. Your food goes from the farm to our Brookvale hub. You collect it Monday to Friday.

No lock-in. No middlemen marking it up. No food sitting in a cold chain for weeks. Just an honest supply chain where the farmer earns a fairer price and your family gets food that is days old, not weeks. Sydney families collect from the Brookvale hub.

sections.heading: The food speaks for itself
sections.body:
100% grass-fed beef from pasture, raised without hormones or feedlots. Wild-caught Alaskan salmon. Pork from Bundarra Farm. Biodynamic dairy from Paris Creek. Eggs from Farmer Brown. Pantry staples from producers we trust.

Every product traces to a named farm. That is the promise we built this business on.

sections.heading: Now you can be part of it
sections.body:
We are launching an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in mid-2026. Minimum investment: $50. Cap: $10,000 per investor.

A $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP access: first in when the campaign opens, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A.

This is early-stage investing. It carries real risk. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal. We can't promise returns — early-stage investing carries real risk.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

creative_brief: Hero: candid shot of founder in a kitchen with family, natural light, warm tones. Below-fold: simple illustrated supply chain showing farm to hub to family. Named farm partner photos. Brookvale hub lifestyle shot. First-person letter tone throughout. Should feel like reading a personal note, not a sales page.
